Name: TIDieR
LongName: Template for Intervention Description and Replication (TIDieR) checklist and guide
Description: TIDieR is a 12-item intervention-reporting checklist and guide designed to improve completeness of intervention descriptions so studies can be interpreted, replicated, and implemented.
Aim: To ensure interventions are described in enough detail for replication and practical implementation, including rationale, materials, procedures, provider details, delivery mode, dose, tailoring, modifications, and fidelity.
Keywords: Template for Intervention Description and Replication

Scope.Coverage: Any manuscript that evaluates or documents one or more interventions and needs a reproducible, implementation-ready intervention description, including trials, protocols, and other intervention-focused designs.
Scope.Focus: Intervention rationale, materials, procedures, provider qualifications, delivery mode, setting, schedule/dose, personalization, protocol modifications, and planned/actual fidelity or adherence.
Scope.ContrastWith: TIDieR is not a full design-level reporting standard by itself; it is an intervention-description overlay intended to be used alongside primary design guidelines such as CONSORT or SPIRIT and other study-design checklists.

primary.description: Hoffmann TC, Glasziou PP, Boutron I, et al. Better reporting of interventions: template for intervention description and replication (TIDieR) checklist and guide. BMJ. 2014;348:g1687.

items.item_question: Is the intervention identified with a clear brief name or phrase?
items.item_question_background: A concise intervention label anchors interpretation and traceability across reports and protocols.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the intervention name/label used by the authors, including comparator labels where applicable.

source_page_refs.quote: BRIEF NAME ... Provide the name or a phrase that describes the intervention.

items.item_question: Is the rationale, theory, or goal of essential intervention elements described?
items.item_question_background: Intervention rationale is needed to interpret mechanisms and transferability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the stated rationale/theory/goals for intervention components.

source_page_refs.quote: WHY ... Describe any rationale, theory, or goal of the elements essential to the intervention.

items.item_question: Are physical/information materials used in intervention delivery or provider training described and locatable?
items.item_question_background: Materials are often required to replicate intervention delivery.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all intervention materials and where they can be accessed (appendix/repository/URL).

source_page_refs.quote: WHAT Materials: Describe any physical or informational materials ... Provide information on where the materials can be accessed.

items.item_question: Are intervention procedures, activities, and processes described, including enabling/support activities?
items.item_question_background: Procedure transparency is central to faithful replication and implementation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract stepwise procedures and any enabling/support processes used in delivery.

source_page_refs.quote: Procedures: Describe each of the procedures, activities, and/or processes used in the intervention...

items.item_question: Are intervention providers' categories, expertise/background, and training described?
items.item_question_background: Provider characteristics can materially affect intervention effects and reproducibility.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract provider roles, qualifications/experience, and specific training for delivery.

source_page_refs.quote: WHO PROVIDED ... For each category of intervention provider ... describe their expertise, background and any specific training given.

items.item_question: Are modes of intervention delivery described (for example face-to-face, internet, telephone) and individual/group format?
items.item_question_background: Delivery mode affects accessibility, fidelity, and comparability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all delivery modes/channels and whether delivery was individual or group-based.

source_page_refs.quote: HOW ... Describe the modes of delivery ... and whether it was provided individually or in a group.

items.item_question: Is the intervention setting/location described, including necessary infrastructure or relevant features?
items.item_question_background: Setting context influences feasibility and external validity.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract intervention location(s), setting type(s), and infrastructure/context features.

source_page_refs.quote: WHERE ... Describe the type(s) of location(s) where the intervention occurred, including any necessary infrastructure...

items.item_question: Are intervention schedule and dose details reported (frequency, period, number of sessions, duration, intensity/dose)?
items.item_question_background: Dose/schedule detail is required for replication and interpretation of outcomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract timing and dose parameters: frequency, schedule, total period, session count, duration, intensity/dose.

source_page_refs.quote: Describe the number of times the intervention was delivered and over what period of time... duration, intensity or dose.

items.item_question: If personalized/titrated/adapted, are what/why/when/how of tailoring described?
items.item_question_background: Personalization rules are essential for reproducibility in adaptive interventions.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether tailoring occurred and the exact adaptation rules, timing, and rationale.

source_page_refs.quote: If the intervention was planned to be personalised, titrated or adapted, then describe what, why, when, and how.

items.item_question: If intervention changed during study, are modifications described (what/why/when/how)?
items.item_question_background: Protocol modifications can influence interpretation and generalizability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all in-study intervention modifications with reasons and timing.

source_page_refs.quote: If the intervention was modified during the course of the study, describe the changes (what, why, when, and how).

items.item_question: If adherence/fidelity was assessed, are planned assessment methods, assessors, and maintenance strategies reported?
items.item_question_background: Planned fidelity assessment helps judge intervention integrity.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract planned adherence/fidelity metrics, assessors, and strategies to maintain/improve fidelity.

source_page_refs.quote: Planned: If intervention adherence or fidelity was assessed, describe how and by whom...

items.item_question: If adherence/fidelity was assessed, is actual intervention delivery relative to plan reported?
items.item_question_background: Actual fidelity determines whether observed effects reflect intended intervention.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract actual adherence/fidelity results and extent intervention was delivered as planned.

source_page_refs.quote: Actual: If intervention adherence or fidelity was assessed, describe the extent to which the intervention was delivered as planned.

Aliases: Template for Intervention Description and Replication
EvidenceExamplesPositive: The intervention is described using TIDieR including materials, procedures, provider training, delivery mode, and dose schedule.
We reported intervention fidelity with planned and actual adherence and described all protocol modifications.
This nonpharmacologic trial uses CONSORT with TIDieR as an extension for intervention details.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: The paper mentions tidy data and tidyverse packages but does not report an intervention study.
This manuscript is a molecular assay protocol with no intervention providers, delivery mode, or fidelity reporting context.
The article is a narrative editorial without intervention procedures or dose/schedule reporting.
Matching.Explain: Adds an intervention-description reporting overlay when manuscripts evaluate interventions and need reproducible details on materials, procedures, delivery, dosing, tailoring, and fidelity.
